goals: Provide `vae_decode_batch` to convert any batched latent (4-D or 5-D) into a flat
Provide `vae_encode_batch` to convert a `list[PIL.Image]` into a batched latent tensor.
All four functions must follow the established `vae.py` patterns: pure duck-typing
All tests pass on CPU-only environments (CI constraint).

userStories.title: Decode a video latent batch into PIL frames
userStories.description: As a Python developer building a video generation pipeline, I want to call `vae_decode_batch(vae, latent)` with a ComfyUI LATENT dict whose `"samples"` tensor is either 4-D `(B, C, H, W)` or 5-D `(B, C, T, H, W)`, so that I get back a flat `list[PIL.Image]` with one image per frame (all batches concatenated) without writing any reshape boilerplate myself.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `vae_decode_batch(vae, {"samples": tensor_4d})` returns a `list[PIL.Image]` of
`vae_decode_batch(vae, {"samples": tensor_5d})` returns a `list[PIL.Image]` of
Shape auto-detection: the function checks `len(tensor.shape)` — no explicit `mode`
The returned list is never empty when the input is valid.
`is_nested` / unbind handling is applied (matching existing `vae_decode` logic).
`detach()` / `cpu()` called on each image slice before conversion (GPU-safety).
Typecheck / lint passes.

userStories.title: Encode a list of PIL images into a video latent batch
userStories.description: As a Python developer building a video generation pipeline, I want to call `vae_encode_batch(vae, images)` with a `list[PIL.Image]`, so that I get back a ComfyUI LATENT dict `{"samples": tensor}` whose shape is appropriate for passing to the sampler (batched 4-D or 5-D, depending on the VAE).

acceptanceCriteria.text: `vae_encode_batch(vae, [img1, img2, img3])` returns a dict with key `"samples"`.
All images are converted to RGB and stacked into a single pixel tensor before
Works with `torch` available (returns real tensor) and without `torch` (returns
Raises `ValueError` when `images` is an empty list.
Typecheck / lint passes.

userStories.title: Tiled decode for large video frames
userStories.description: As a Python developer generating high-resolution video, I want `vae_decode_batch_tiled(vae, latent, tile_size, overlap)`, so that I can decode large batched latents frame-by-frame without running out of GPU memory.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Signature: `vae_decode_batch_tiled(vae, latent, tile_size=512, overlap=64)` —
Decodes every frame slice using `vae.decode_tiled(...)` and collects results into
Handles both 4-D and 5-D tensors (same auto-detection logic as US-001).
`_VaeDecoderTiled` protocol is reused (no new protocol class needed).
Typecheck / lint passes.

userStories.title: Tiled encode for large video frames
userStories.description: As a Python developer generating high-resolution video, I want `vae_encode_batch_tiled(vae, images, tile_size, overlap)`, so that I can encode large PIL frames into a batched latent without OOM.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Signature: `vae_encode_batch_tiled(vae, images, tile_size=512, overlap=64)`.
Each image is encoded individually via `vae.encode_tiled(...)` and results are
Raises `ValueError` when `images` is an empty list.
`_VaeEncoderTiled` protocol is reused (no new protocol class needed).
Typecheck / lint passes.

userStories.title: Public API export
userStories.description: As a Python developer, I want to import all four batch functions from `pycomfy.vae`, so that my import lines are consistent with the rest of pycomfy's API.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `from pycomfy.vae import vae_decode_batch, vae_encode_batch` works without error.
`from pycomfy.vae import vae_decode_batch_tiled, vae_encode_batch_tiled` works
All four names are added to `__all__` in `pycomfy/vae.py`.
All four names are imported in `pycomfy/__init__.py` and added to its `__all__`
`from pycomfy import vae_decode_batch, vae_encode_batch, vae_decode_batch_tiled, vae_encode_batch_tiled`
Existing `vae_decode`, `vae_decode_tiled`, `vae_encode`, `vae_encode_tiled` remain
Typecheck / lint passes.

userStories.title: Tests for batch functions
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want pytest tests covering the four new batch functions on CPU, so that CI can validate correctness without a GPU.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `vae_decode_batch` tested with a mock VAE and a fake 4-D tensor → list of 1+ PIL
`vae_decode_batch` tested with a fake 5-D tensor → correct frame count.
`vae_encode_batch` tested with a list of small PIL images → dict with `"samples"`.
`vae_encode_batch` raises `ValueError` on empty list.
`vae_decode_batch_tiled` tested with a mock tiled VAE.
`vae_encode_batch_tiled` tested with a mock tiled VAE.
All existing VAE tests still pass (`uv run pytest` green).

functionalRequirements.description: `vae_decode_batch(vae, latent)` — accepts a `_VaeDecoder` duck-type and a
`vae_decode_batch_tiled(vae, latent, tile_size=512, overlap=64)` — same as FR-1
`vae_encode_batch_tiled(vae, images, tile_size=512, overlap=64)` — same as FR-2
All four functions must defer `import torch` to call time (lazy import pattern).
